description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two prominent metallic hoops. The wood appears weathered and textured, with visible grain and subtle color variations, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The metallic bands are a dark, tarnished brass or bronze color, fastened with round rivets, and provide structural reinforcement. The top of the barrel is open, revealing a hollow interior, and there is no lid or cover present. There are no visible handles or grips, and the surface is free from any labels, markings, or decorative elements. The overall appearance suggests it is designed for storage or transport of goods, commonly found in urban, warehouse, or outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with two dark metallic hoops and an open top, showing no handles or markings. The surface is textured and aged, suitable for storage or decorative use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, two metallic hoops, and visible rivets on the upper band.
description_view_1: Side view with both metallic hoops visible, no rivets or markings apparent on this side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a clear view of the wood grain and both metallic hoops, no rivets visible.
description_view_3: Rear view with both hoops visible and a single rivet on the upper band, wood texture prominent.
